app.title: MSG Viewer
welcome.title: .msg file viewer
png.target.title: Export to PNG
png.target.body: How do you want to export the message image?
attachments.title: Attachments
attachments.dragHint: drag it out to save it
png.tooTall.title: Message is too long
png.tooTall.body: The content is {h} px tall and the PNG limit is {max} px. You can export truncated, or use PDF for long emails.
error.title: Could not open file
error.not-found: The file does not exist or cannot be read.
error.not-cfbf: Not an Outlook message: no CFBF structure found (renamed extension?).
error.truncated: The file is incomplete or truncated and cannot be read.
error.encrypted: The message is S/MIME encrypted and its content cannot be displayed.
error.unsupported-class: Unsupported Outlook item type: {detail}. Only email messages are supported.
error.too-large: The file exceeds the maximum supported size.
error.parse-error: The file could not be interpreted as an Outlook message.
header.subject: Subject
actions.copy: Copy the selection with formatting (or the whole body if nothing is selected)
about.detail:
Lightweight, 100% offline viewer for Outlook .msg files on Linux and macOS.
Your mail never leaves your machine: no network, no telemetry, no clouds.

© 2026 Oscar Henríquez · GPL-3.0 License
about.eggDetail:
Every .msg file is actually a tiny 1991 filesystem (CFBF): sectors, directories and even its own FAT… inside an email.

Technically, you've been receiving floppy disks disguised as emails for years. 💾
associate.title: Associate file types
associate.intro: Choose which file types open with MSG Viewer on double-click:
associate.macIntro: On macOS, set the association from Finder (one time): right-click a file → Get Info → "Open with" → MSG Viewer → "Change All…".
assocOffer.title: Open email files with MSG Viewer?
assocOffer.body: .msg, .eml and .emlx files don't open with MSG Viewer yet. Do you want to associate them so they open on double-click?
toast.bodyEmpty: The message has no content to copy
linkwarn.bodyOn: Links whose visible text shows a domain different from the real destination will be highlighted, to help you spot phishing attempts.
linkwarn.bodyOff: Links whose text shows a different domain than the real one will no longer be highlighted. You can re-enable it with the same button.
leave.title: Leaving the viewer
leave.body: You are about to open an external link in your browser:
leave.warn: Make sure you trust this address before continuing.
leave.mismatchExplain: A misleading link shows a trusted-looking domain but sends you somewhere else to steal your data (phishing). Don't continue unless you recognize the real destination.
remoteImg.title: Load the remote image?
remoteImg.body: This image is not embedded in the email; it would be downloaded from an external server:
remoteImg.warn: Loading it lets that server record your IP address, the exact date and time you opened the email, and basic details about your device. This is the technique (tracking pixel) senders and spam use to confirm your account is active and to learn whether you read their messages.
remoteImg.error: Could not load the remote image
remoteImg.tooLarge: The remote image is too large
remoteImg.notImage: The address did not return an image
leave.homographExplain: The real domain uses letters from another alphabet (Cyrillic/Greek) that mimic Latin ones to impersonate a trusted site. Don't trust it unless you recognize the destination.
unlink.title: Disable links in this message
unlink.body: For your safety, all links in this message will be disabled to protect you from phishing or malware. You can re-enable them with the same button.
